title: QFT & Non-Perturbative Phenomena Shadow Analysis

instanton_amplitude.why: exp(iθ): complex phase from θ-vacuum angle = EML-3

BPST_solution.description: A_μ^a = 2η_{aμν}x_ν/(x²+ρ²): real, but generates complex topological charge
BPST_solution.why: Q = ∫Tr(F∧F)/(8π²) ∈ Z: integer charge with complex phase e^{iQθ} = EML-3

theta_superposition.description: |θ⟩ = Σ_n exp(inθ)|n⟩: superposition over topological sectors
theta_superposition.why: exp(inθ): complex Fourier sum over winding numbers = EML-3

CP_violation.why: θ ≠ 0 breaks CP: complex oscillation in vacuum structure = EML-3

anomalous_dimensions.description: γ*(g*) at fixed point g*: non-perturbative anomalous dimension
anomalous_dimensions.why: Non-perturbative g* means g* = exp(-1/β₀g) type: but at fixed point, operator dimensions involve exp(iπγ) phases = EML-3

operator_spectrum.description: Δ = d + γ*(g*): operator dimension at non-trivial fixed point
operator_spectrum.why: γ* non-perturbative → involves complex saddle points in path integral = EML-3

chiral_condensate.description: ⟨ψ̄ψ⟩ ≈ -(250 MeV)³: order parameter for chiral symmetry breaking
chiral_condensate.why: Condensate = real-valued expectation value = EML-2 (thermodynamic average)

gluon_condensate.why: Real-valued OPE coefficient: power correction to correlators = EML-2

condensates.note: Condensates have EML-2 shadow: they are real-valued order parameters (measurement)

asymptotic_freedom.object: QCD Landau pole / asymptotic freedom boundary

running_coupling.description: αs(μ) = 1/(β₀ log(μ²/Λ_QCD²)): Landau pole at μ=Λ_QCD
running_coupling.why: 1/log is EML-2 (ratio with log); pole = EML-∞; shadow via log = EML-2

\u039bQCD_scale.why: exp(1/αs) with 1/αs = β₀·log: nested exp+log = EML-2 shadow

monopole_charge.description: Dirac monopole: g = 2π/e, Dirac string phase = exp(ie∮A·dl)
monopole_charge.why: Dirac phase factor = complex exponential = EML-3

vortex_winding.why: exp(inθ): winding number phase = complex oscillation = EML-3

key_theorem: The QFT Non-Perturbative Shadow Rule (S261): Non-perturbative QFT phenomena split by shadow type: TOPOLOGICAL (winding numbers, phases) → EML-3 shadow:   instantons (e^{iθ}), θ-vacuum (e^{inθ}), solitons (e^{inθ}), monopoles (e^{ie∮A}). NON-TOPOLOGICAL (real-valued condensates, running coupling) → EML-2 shadow:   ⟨ψ̄ψ⟩ (real order parameter), Λ_QCD (exp(-1/g) with real argument),   gluon condensate (real OPE coefficient). THE RULE: shadow depth = 3 iff the non-perturbative phenomenon carries an irreducible complex phase (topological angle, winding, Dirac string). shadow depth = 2 iff the phenomenon is real-valued (condensate, scale). This rule distinguishes the TWO types of non-perturbative physics: phase-carrying (topological, EML-3) vs magnitude-only (condensate, EML-2). Both are EML-∞ (non-perturbative = beyond any finite loop order), but their shadows encode which mathematical tools approach them.
rabbit_hole_log: Topological → EML-3 shadow: instantons, θ-vacuum, solitons all carry exp(iθ) phases
Condensates → EML-2 shadow: ⟨ψ̄ψ⟩ and gluon condensate are real-valued
RULE: shadow=3 iff irreducible complex phase; shadow=2 iff real-valued non-perturbative
RG fixed points → EML-3: non-perturbative anomalous dimensions involve complex saddles
Asymptotic freedom → EML-2: αs(μ) ~ 1/log(μ²/Λ²) is real-valued
